best-free-online-pdf-compressors.en:
If you've ever tried to email a PDF and got the dreaded 'file too large' message, you know the pain. PDF compression isn't just about shrinking files — it's about keeping your workflow moving.

We tested 20+ free online PDF compressors with files of various sizes (10MB, 50MB, and 100MB scanned documents). Here are the 5 that actually work.

Quick Comparison Table

ToolMax SizeCompression RatioSpeedPrivacy
pixelpdf.win50MB70-85%Fast✅ Auto-delete
iLovePDF10MB60-75%Fast⚠️ Manual delete
Smallpdf5MB50-65%Medium⚠️ Manual delete
PDF Compressor (by PDFCandy)100MB55-70%Medium✅ Auto-delete
YouCompress200MB40-60%Slow✅ Auto-delete

1. pixelpdf.win — Best All-Round Free Option

pixelpdf gives you the best balance of compression ratio, speed, and privacy. Files are automatically deleted after processing. It handles up to 50MB files and consistently achieves 70-85% compression on scanned documents.

No sign-up required, no watermarks, no daily limits. It's our top pick for regular use.

2. iLovePDF — Best for Batch Processing

iLovePDF is a well-known name in the PDF space. Its compression tool is solid, though the free tier limits you to 10MB files and 1 task per hour. For batch processing, you'll need to pay ($12/month).

3. Smallpdf — Good but Limited Free Tier

Smallpdf offers reliable compression with a clean interface. The catch: the free version only handles 2 files per day. Great for occasional use, frustrating if you need it regularly.

4. PDFCandy — Best for Large Files

If you regularly work with files over 50MB, PDFCandy's compressor supports up to 100MB for free. The compression ratio isn't as aggressive as pixelpdf, but it's a solid backup option.

5. YouCompress — Unlimited Size, Slower Speed

YouCompress accepts files up to 200MB for free — the most generous limit on this list. The trade-off is speed: large files can take several minutes to process. Privacy is good with auto-deletion.

Which One Should You Use?

For everyday PDF compression, pixelpdf.win is the clear winner — fast compression, good ratios, and no privacy headaches. Keep PDFCandy as a backup for oversized files.

None of these tools require installation, and all work in your browser. That's the beauty of online tools — they're there when you need them, gone when you don't.

best-free-color-palette-generators.en:
Whether you're a web designer, a developer, or just someone who wants their slides to look decent, a good color palette generator is essential. We compared 7 of the most popular options to find which ones save you real time.

How We Tested

We evaluated each tool on three criteria: speed (how fast can you get a usable palette), variety (how many different color schemes can it produce), and export options (can you copy HEX codes easily).

The Results

1. Coolors — The fastest palette generator we tested. Press the space bar to generate new palettes instantly. Great for quick inspiration. Free for basic use.

2. Adobe Color — Best for understanding color theory. Lets you pick a color harmony rule (complementary, analogous, triadic) and builds palettes systematically. Free with Adobe account.

3. Canva Color Palette Generator — Upload an image and Canva extracts the dominant colors. Perfect for branding projects where you need to match existing assets.

4. exdreamcolors.win — A no-fuss color picker with HEX/RGB/HSL converters built in. Nothing fancy, but when you just need to grab a color code and move on, it's the fastest option.

5. ColorHunt — A curated collection of beautiful color palettes. Not a generator per se, but great for finding pre-made palettes that designers have already tested.

6. Paletton — The most advanced color scheme designer. You can fine-tune every parameter. Steep learning curve but incredibly powerful.

7. ColorSpace — Enter one color and it generates 20+ palettes automatically. Great for when you have a brand color and need complementary options fast.

Verdict

For quick palette generation, use Coolors. For understanding color theory, use Adobe Color. For a lightweight color tool you can keep open in a tab all day, exdreamcolors.win is a solid choice.

best-free-ai-image-generators.en:
AI image generators have exploded in the past year. What used to require expensive subscriptions now has solid free alternatives. But not all free generators are created equal — some nail photorealism, others excel at illustration, and a few are just frustrating to use.

We tested the most popular free AI image generators in June 2026. Here's what actually works.

Quick Comparison

ToolFree GenerationsBest ForResolutionSpeed
Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3)15/dayPhotorealism, product mockups1024×1024~10s
Leonardo AI150/dayGame assets, concept art1024×1024~5s
Stable Diffusion (via Clipdrop)100/dayArtistic styles, custom models2048×2048~3s
Ideogram25/dayText in images, logos1024×1024~8s
Playground AI50/dayMixed media, editing existing images1024×1024~6s

1. Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3) — Best for Photorealism

Microsoft Designer gives you access to DALL-E 3 for free — 15 boosted generations per day (fast queue), plus unlimited slower generations. The image quality is top-tier, especially for realistic scenes and product mockups.

Pros

  • Excellent photorealism and prompt understanding
  • Built-in editing tools let you erase and regenerate parts of images
  • Free tier is genuinely useful — 15 daily credits covers most casual needs
  • Commercial use allowed for free generations

Cons

  • 15 daily generations fills up fast for power users
  • Requires a Microsoft account
  • No API access or batch generation
  • Some prompts get blocked by content filters

Who it's for: Marketers, bloggers, and small business owners who need a few high-quality images per day for social media, blog posts, or product listings.

2. Leonardo AI — Best for Game Assets and Concept Art

Leonardo AI started as a tool for game developers and it still shows. The interface is built around creating assets — characters, environments, items. The free tier gives you a generous 150 tokens per day (about 30-50 generations depending on settings).

Pros

  • Very generous free tier — 150 tokens resets daily
  • Specialized models for different art styles (anime, cinematic, 3D)
  • Real-time image generation canvas
  • Community gallery for inspiration and remixing

Cons

  • Interface has a learning curve — not as beginner-friendly
  • Photorealism lags behind DALL-E 3
  • Some features locked behind paid plans (upscaling, transparent backgrounds)
  • Commercial terms are somewhat restrictive for free users

Who it's for: Game developers, digital artists, and anyone creating assets in bulk who needs variety more than photorealism.

3. Stable Diffusion (via Clipdrop) — Best for Custom Styles

Stability AI's Clipdrop gives you access to the latest Stable Diffusion models with a clean, web-based interface. The free tier offers 100 generations per day at impressive speed — most images render in under 3 seconds.

Pros

  • Blazing fast generation — under 3 seconds per image
  • Highest resolution output (up to 2048×2048 for free)
  • Excellent for artistic and stylized images
  • Background removal and relighting tools included

Cons

  • Prompt understanding is weaker than DALL-E 3
  • Hands and faces can still look weird in complex poses
  • No fine-tuning or model customization on free tier
  • Watermark on free generations (small, bottom-right)

Who it's for: Artists and designers who value speed and custom styles, and don't mind tweaking prompts to get the right output.

4. Ideogram — Best for Text in Images

If you've ever tried to generate an image with text in it using AI, you know it's usually a mess. Ideogram solves this — it's the best tool we've found for rendering readable text inside AI-generated images. Logos, posters, memes — Ideogram handles them all.

Pros

  • Unmatched text rendering in generated images
  • Great for logos, posters, and social media graphics
  • Clean, intuitive interface
  • Good art style variety

Cons

  • Only 25 free generations per day
  • Slower than competitors (8+ seconds)
  • Less control over composition than other tools
  • Content filters can be aggressive

Who it's for: Anyone who needs text in their AI images — logo designers, social media managers, and meme creators.

5. Playground AI — Best for Image Editing

Playground AI stands out because it's not just a generator — it's an editor. You can upload an existing image, select an area, and tell the AI what to change. Think of it as Photoshop's generative fill, but free.

Pros

  • Unique image editing capabilities — modify existing images with prompts
  • 50 free generations per day
  • Good for mixed media and collage-style work
  • Active community sharing designs and prompts

Cons

  • Base generation quality is behind DALL-E 3 and Leonardo
  • Interface can feel cluttered
  • Output resolution limited on free tier
  • Some advanced editing tools are paywalled

Who it's for: Content creators who need to edit and remix existing images — think thumbnail designers, meme makers, and social media creators.

Which One Should You Use?

Your choice comes down to what you're creating:

  • Need photorealistic images for marketing? Start with Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3).
  • Making game assets or concept art? Leonardo AI's 150 daily tokens are hard to beat.
  • Want the fastest workflow? Clipdrop's Stable Diffusion renders images in 3 seconds.
  • Need text in your images? Ideogram is the only tool that gets this right.
  • Editing existing images? Playground AI's edit features are unmatched.

Our recommendation: use Microsoft Designer for your "hero"images and keep Clipdrop open in another tab for quick iterations and variations. Between those two, you'll cover 90% of what most people need from an AI image generator — all without spending a dollar.

FAQ

Are AI-generated images copyright-free?

It depends on the tool and your usage. Most free tiers grant you personal and commercial use rights, but the legal landscape is still evolving. Check each tool's terms before using images commercially. Microsoft Designer allows commercial use of free generations as of June 2026.

Which free AI image generator has no daily limit?

None of the good ones are truly unlimited on the free tier. The most generous is Leonardo AI with 150 tokens/day (roughly 30-50 images). For completely unlimited generation, you'd need to run Stable Diffusion locally on your own hardware, which requires a decent GPU.

Can I use AI-generated images for my business?

Yes, for most of these tools. Microsoft Designer and Clipdrop allow commercial use on the free tier. Leonardo AI's free tier has some restrictions — read their terms carefully if you're selling assets. Always keep screenshots of the terms at the time of generation.

Why do AI generators struggle with hands?

AI models are trained on millions of images, but hands appear in relatively few positions and angles compared to faces or landscapes. This makes hands statistically harder to reproduce accurately. Newer models like DALL-E 3 and Stable Diffusion 3 have gotten much better, but they still slip up occasionally.

What's the best free alternative to Midjourney?

Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3) comes closest in terms of image quality and photorealism. Leonardo AI matches Midjourney's creative flexibility. The truth is Midjourney is still slightly better overall, but the gap has narrowed dramatically — for most projects, the free alternatives are more than good enough.
best-free-ai-image-generators.ja:
AI画像生成ツールはここ1年で爆発的に増えました。以前は高額なサブスクリプションが必要でしたが、今では優れた無料の選択肢があります。2026年6月時点で最も人気のある無料AI画像生成ツールをテストしました。

比較表

ツール無料生成数得意分野解像度速度
Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3)15回/日フォトリアル、製品モックアップ1024×1024約10秒
Leonardo AI150トークン/日ゲーム素材、コンセプトアート1024×1024約5秒
Stable Diffusion (Clipdrop)100回/日アートスタイル、カスタムモデル2048×2048約3秒
Ideogram25回/日画像内テキスト、ロゴ1024×1024約8秒
Playground AI50回/日画像編集、ミックスメディア1024×1024約6秒

1. Microsoft Designer — フォトリアルに最適

Microsoft DesignerはDALL-E 3に無料でアクセスできます。1日15回の高速生成に加え、無制限の低速生成も利用可能です。画像品質は最高クラスで、特にリアルなシーンや製品モックアップに優れています。

メリット

  • 卓越したフォトリアル品質とプロンプト理解力
  • 画像編集ツール内蔵（部分消去・再生成）
  • 無料枠でも十分実用的
  • 商用利用可能

デメリット

  • 1日15回ではヘビーユーザーには不足
  • Microsoftアカウントが必要
  • API・バッチ生成不可

こんな人に: マーケター、ブロガー、小規模ビジネスオーナー。

2. Leonardo AI — ゲーム素材とコンセプトアートに最適

Leonardo AIはゲーム開発者向けに作られたツールです。無料枠は1日150トークンと非常に寛大で、設定によって30〜50枚の画像を生成できます。

メリット

  • 150トークン/日と寛大な無料枠
  • アニメ・シネマティック・3Dなど特化モデル
  • リアルタイム生成キャンバス
  • コミュニティギャラリーでリミックス可能

デメリット

  • インターフェースがやや複雑
  • フォトリアル品質はDALL-E 3に劣る
  • 一部機能は有料プラン限定

こんな人に: ゲーム開発者、デジタルアーティスト。

3. Stable Diffusion (Clipdrop) — カスタムスタイルに最適

Stability AIのClipdropは最新のStable Diffusionモデルにウェブブラウザからアクセスできます。無料枠は1日100回、生成速度は3秒未満と驚異的です。

メリット

  • 3秒未満の超高速生成
  • 無料で2048×2048の高解像度出力
  • 背景削除・リライティングツール付き

デメリット

  • プロンプト理解力はDALL-E 3に劣る
  • 手足の描写に難あり
  • 無料版にはウォーターマーク

こんな人に: スピード重視のアーティスト、デザイナー。

4. Ideogram — 画像内テキストに最適

AIでテキスト入りの画像を生成しようとして文字化けした経験はありませんか？Ideogramはこの問題を見事に解決します。ロゴ、ポスター、ミームなど、テキストを含む画像生成では最高のツールです。

メリット

  • 画像内テキストの品質が圧倒的
  • ロゴ・ポスター・SNSグラフィックに最適
  • 直感的なインターフェース

デメリット

  • 1日25回のみ
  • 生成速度がやや遅い
  • 構図のコントロールに制限あり

こんな人に: ロゴデザイナー、SNS運用者、ミームクリエイター。

どれを選ぶべき？

マーケティング用のフォトリアル画像ならMicrosoft Designer、ゲーム素材ならLeonardo AI、スピード重視ならClipdrop、テキスト入り画像ならIdeogramです。二つ選ぶならMicrosoft DesignerとClipdropの組み合わせが最強です。

FAQ

AI生成画像の著作権は？

ツールによって異なります。Microsoft Designerは商用利用可能です。各ツールの利用規約を確認してください。

完全無制限の無料AI画像生成ツールは？

無料で完全無制限の優れたツールはありません。最も寛大なのはLeonardo AIの150トークン/日です。完全無制限が必要なら、自分のPCでStable Diffusionをローカル実行する必要があります。

ビジネスに使えますか？

Microsoft DesignerとClipdropは無料枠でも商用利用可能です。Leonardo AIの無料枠には制限があります。
best-free-ai-image-generators.de:
KI-Bildgeneratoren haben im letzten Jahr einen enormen Aufschwung erlebt. Was früher teure Abonnements erforderte, ist heute mit soliden kostenlosen Alternativen möglich. Wir haben die beliebtesten kostenlosen KI-Bildgeneratoren im Juni 2026 getestet.

Vergleichstabelle

ToolKostenlose GenerierungenAm besten fürAuflösungGeschwindigkeit
Microsoft Designer (DALL-E 3)15/TagFotorealismus, Produkt-Mockups1024×1024~10s
Leonardo AI150/TagSpiel-Assets, Concept Art1024×1024~5s
Stable Diffusion (Clipdrop)100/TagKünstlerische Stile, Custom Models2048×2048~3s
Ideogram25/TagText in Bildern, Logos1024×1024~8s
Playground AI50/TagBildbearbeitung, Mixed Media1024×1024~6s

1. Microsoft Designer — Bester Fotorealismus

Microsoft Designer bietet kostenlosen Zugang zu DALL-E 3 — 15 beschleunigte Generationen pro Tag plus unbegrenzte langsamere. Die Bildqualität ist erstklassig, besonders für realistische Szenen und Produkt-Mockups.

Vorteile

  • Hervorragender Fotorealismus und Prompt-Verständnis
  • Integrierte Bearbeitungswerkzeuge
  • Kostenlose Stufe ist wirklich nützlich
  • Kommerzielle Nutzung erlaubt

Nachteile

  • 15 tägliche Generationen für Power-User knapp
  • Microsoft-Konto erforderlich
  • Kein API-Zugang

Für wen: Vermarkter, Blogger, Kleinunternehmer.

2. Leonardo AI — Beste für Spiel-Assets

Leonardo AI wurde für Spieleentwickler entwickelt. Die kostenlose Stufe bietet großzügige 150 Token pro Tag (ca. 30-50 Generationen).

Vorteile

  • Sehr großzügige kostenlose Stufe — 150 Token täglich
  • Spezialisierte Modelle für verschiedene Kunststile
  • Echtzeit-Generierungsleinwand
  • Community-Galerie

Nachteile

  • Lernkurve bei der Benutzeroberfläche
  • Fotorealismus hinter DALL-E 3
  • Einige Funktionen nur in Bezahlplänen

Für wen: Spieleentwickler, digitale Künstler.

3. Stable Diffusion (Clipdrop) — Beste für Custom Styles

Clipdrop von Stability AI bietet Zugang zu den neuesten Stable Diffusion Modellen über eine saubere Weboberfläche. 100 kostenlose Generationen pro Tag mit beeindruckender Geschwindigkeit — die meisten Bilder in unter 3 Sekunden.

Vorteile

  • Extrem schnelle Generierung — unter 3 Sekunden
  • Höchste Auflösung (bis 2048×2048 kostenlos)
  • Hintergrundentfernung und Relighting inklusive

Nachteile

  • Prompt-Verständnis schwächer als DALL-E 3
  • Hände und Gesichter manchmal problematisch
  • Wasserzeichen auf kostenlosen Generationen

Für wen: Künstler und Designer, die Wert auf Geschwindigkeit legen.

4. Ideogram — Beste für Text in Bildern

Ideogram löst das Problem, das die meisten KI-Generatoren haben: lesbaren Text in Bildern zu rendern. Perfekt für Logos, Poster und Memes.

Vorteile

  • Unübertroffene Textdarstellung in generierten Bildern
  • Ideal für Logos und Social-Media-Grafiken
  • Intuitive Benutzeroberfläche

Nachteile

  • Nur 25 kostenlose Generationen pro Tag
  • Langsamer als Konkurrenten
  • Weniger Kompositionskontrolle

Für wen: Logo-Designer, Social-Media-Manager.

Fazit

Für Marketing-Bilder: Microsoft Designer. Für Spiel-Assets: Leonardo AI. Für Geschwindigkeit: Clipdrop. Für Text in Bildern: Ideogram. Die beste Kombination: Microsoft Designer für Hauptbilder plus Clipdrop für schnelle Variationen.

FAQ

Sind KI-generierte Bilder urheberrechtsfrei?

Das hängt vom Tool ab. Microsoft Designer erlaubt kommerzielle Nutzung. Prüfen Sie die Nutzungsbedingungen jedes Tools.

Welcher Generator hat kein Tageslimit?

Keiner der guten ist wirklich unbegrenzt. Am großzügigsten ist Leonardo AI mit 150 Token/Tag.

Kann ich KI-Bilder geschäftlich nutzen?

Ja, mit Microsoft Designer und Clipdrop auch in der kostenlosen Stufe.

Ventajas

  • Generación ultrarrápida — menos de 3 segundos
  • Mayor resolución de salida (hasta 2048×2048 gratis)
  • Eliminación de fondo y reiluminación incluidas

Desventajas

  • Comprensión de prompts inferior a DALL-E 3
  • Manos y caras a veces problemáticas
  • Marca de agua en generaciones gratuitas

Para quién: Artistas y diseñadores que valoran la velocidad.

4. Ideogram — Mejor para texto en imágenes

Ideogram resuelve el problema que la mayoría de los generadores IA tienen: renderizar texto legible dentro de las imágenes. Perfecto para logos, pósteres y memes.

Ventajas

  • Renderizado de texto inigualable en imágenes generadas
  • Ideal para logos y gráficos de redes sociales
  • Interfaz intuitiva

Desventajas

  • Solo 25 generaciones gratuitas al día
  • Más lento que la competencia
  • Menos control sobre la composición

Para quién: Diseñadores de logos, gestores de redes sociales.

Conclusión

Para imágenes de marketing: Microsoft Designer. Para assets de juegos: Leonardo AI. Para velocidad: Clipdrop. Para texto en imágenes: Ideogram. La mejor combinación: Microsoft Designer para imágenes principales más Clipdrop para variaciones rápidas.

FAQ

¿Las imágenes generadas por IA tienen derechos de autor?

Depende de la herramienta. Microsoft Designer permite uso comercial. Revisa los términos de cada herramienta.

¿Cuál no tiene límite diario?

Ninguno de los buenos es ilimitado. El más generoso es Leonardo AI con 150 tokens/día.

¿Puedo usar imágenes IA para mi negocio?

Sí, con Microsoft Designer y Clipdrop incluso en el nivel gratuito.

best-free-screen-recording-tools.en:
Free screen recorders are everywhere, but most people only discover the tradeoffs after wasting an afternoon with the wrong one. Some tools are excellent for tutorial videos but clumsy for game capture. Others are perfect for quick team updates but too limited for long-form recording. We tested the most popular free screen recording tools in 2026 to see which ones are actually worth keeping installed.

The short version: there is no single best choice for everyone. OBS Studio is still the strongest free option for power users. Loom is the easiest way to record and share quick updates. ShareX is unbeatable for Windows users who want automation and screenshots in the same workflow. The right pick depends on what you record most often.

Quick Comparison Table

ToolBest ForPlatformFree LimitsEase of Use
OBS StudioStreaming, tutorials, gamingWindows, Mac, LinuxNo recording limitMedium
LoomQuick demos, async team updatesWeb, Windows, Mac, iOS, AndroidShorter videos and limited library on free planVery easy
ShareXWindows workflows, annotated capturesWindowsNo recording limitMedium
ScreenPalSimple training clipsWeb, Windows, Mac, ChromebookWatermark and feature limits on free planEasy
Xbox Game BarCasual gameplay and app captureWindowsBuilt into WindowsVery easy
ClipchampRecord and edit in one placeWeb, WindowsExport and premium template limitsEasy

How We Tested These Tools

We compared each recorder across five things that actually matter in daily use:

  • Setup speed: how fast a beginner can install it and record the first clip
  • Recording flexibility: full screen, window, webcam, microphone, system audio
  • Output quality: whether the final video looks clean without weird compression
  • Sharing workflow: how easy it is to trim, export, or send the result
  • Free plan friction: watermarks, time caps, account requirements, and locked features

We used the same scenarios for every tool: a 5-minute tutorial recording, a quick talking-head demo with webcam overlay, and a longer screen capture with multiple windows open.

1. OBS Studio, Best Free Recorder for Serious Work

OBS Studio is still the most capable free screen recorder by a wide margin. It can capture full displays, individual windows, webcams, microphones, browser sources, and layered scenes. If you want to build polished tutorials, record webinars, or capture gameplay at high quality, OBS is the tool to beat.

The downside is obvious the moment you open it: the interface is not built for total beginners. Scenes, sources, audio mixers, and output settings make sense once you learn them, but the first 15 minutes can feel heavy. That said, the payoff is huge. Once you set up your scene correctly, OBS becomes reliable and repeatable.

Pros

  • No watermark and no recording time limit
  • Excellent video and audio quality
  • Supports advanced scenes, overlays, and multiple sources
  • Great for streaming and local recording in the same app
  • Works on Windows, Mac, and Linux

Cons

  • Steeper learning curve than simpler tools
  • Interface can overwhelm first-time users
  • No built-in cloud sharing workflow
  • Editing features are minimal, so you may need a separate editor

Who it’s for: YouTubers, course creators, streamers, and anyone who records frequently enough to benefit from a more professional setup.

2. Loom, Best for Fast Team Communication

Loom solves a different problem. It is not trying to be a production studio. It is trying to help you explain something quickly, send the link, and move on. That makes it one of the best free screen recorders for remote teams, client updates, bug reports, and internal training clips.

The best part of Loom is speed. Open the app or extension, choose screen plus camera or just screen, hit record, and share the link when you finish. There is almost no friction. For people who send lots of async video messages, that matters more than advanced encoding settings.

Pros

  • Fastest record-and-share workflow we tested
  • Very beginner-friendly interface
  • Good webcam and microphone integration
  • Cloud links make collaboration easy
  • Useful for sales, support, and internal communication

Cons

  • Free plan has storage or video-length limits depending on the current offer
  • Less control over recording settings than OBS
  • Not ideal for high-performance game capture
  • Requires account-based workflow

Who it’s for: Managers, freelancers, support teams, and founders who care more about clarity and speed than production-level control.

3. ShareX, Best for Windows Power Users

ShareX is one of those tools that looks modest at first and then keeps surprising you. It can capture screenshots, scrolling pages, GIFs, short screen recordings, and annotated images. For Windows users who already live in a keyboard-shortcut-heavy workflow, ShareX is incredibly efficient.

Its screen recording workflow is not as polished as Loom and not as studio-grade as OBS, but it fills an important middle ground. You can record fast, annotate quickly, upload automatically, and keep everything moving without touching five separate apps.

Pros

  • Free, open-source, and extremely capable
  • Excellent screenshot and annotation features
  • Automation options save a lot of time
  • Custom upload destinations and workflows
  • Lightweight once configured

Cons

  • Windows only
  • Interface feels technical in places
  • Video editing is limited
  • Can take time to configure well

Who it’s for: Windows users who want screenshots, GIFs, and recordings in one toolbox, especially developers, QA testers, and technical support teams.

4. ScreenPal, Best for Simple Training Videos

ScreenPal sits in the friendly middle. It is easier to start with than OBS, and it offers more education-oriented features than bare-bones recorders. If you need to record tutorials, student walkthroughs, or internal process videos, it is a practical choice.

The free tier is usable, but you will notice the usual limitations: some export restrictions, branded output in certain modes, and premium editing features kept behind the paywall. For occasional training clips, that may be fine. For heavier use, many people outgrow it.

Pros

  • Easy for beginners to understand
  • Built with tutorial and presentation use cases in mind
  • Offers basic editing and narration tools
  • Works across several devices and browsers

Cons

  • Free plan limitations are noticeable
  • Watermark or branding may appear depending on workflow
  • Less flexible than OBS for advanced scenes
  • Can feel slower than lighter tools

Who it’s for: Teachers, trainers, and small teams making straightforward instructional videos.

5. Xbox Game Bar, Best Built-In Option for Windows

If you are on Windows and want the fastest path to “just record this app,” Xbox Game Bar is surprisingly useful. Press the shortcut, start capture, and you are done. No separate installation, no account creation, and no complicated setup.

Its limitations matter though. It is mainly meant for app and gameplay capture, not full desktop workflows with layered scenes. You also get less flexibility around recording layouts, which makes it weaker for professional tutorials.

Pros

  • Built into Windows
  • Very easy to launch and use
  • Good for casual gameplay and single-app recording
  • No extra software to learn

Cons

  • Not ideal for multi-window tutorial setups
  • Feature set is limited
  • Not as flexible as dedicated tools
  • Windows only

Who it’s for: Windows users who need quick recordings and do not want to install anything extra.

6. Clipchamp, Best if You Want Recording Plus Editing

Clipchamp is a solid pick for people who prefer an all-in-one flow. You can record your screen, bring in webcam footage, and trim the final result in the same interface. For social content, short explainers, and lightweight tutorials, that convenience is worth a lot.

The tradeoff is that some useful export and design features are pushed toward paid plans. Still, for basic projects, the free version can cover the whole journey from capture to publish.

Pros

  • Recording and editing in one tool
  • Clean interface
  • Good for short-form content and simple explainers
  • Works well for beginners

Cons

  • Some premium assets and exports are locked
  • Not as powerful as OBS for advanced capture
  • Web workflow may feel slower on weaker machines

Who it’s for: Beginners, marketers, and solo creators who want a simple all-in-one editor-recorder.

Best Picks by Use Case

Use CaseBest ChoiceWhy
Tutorial videosOBS StudioBest quality and scene control
Quick team updatesLoomFastest sharing workflow
Gaming captureOBS StudioReliable high-performance recording
Windows productivity workflowShareXScreenshots, GIFs, and recordings together
Beginner training videosScreenPalSimple setup and easy narration flow
No-install Windows optionXbox Game BarAlready built in

How to Choose the Right Free Screen Recorder

Use this simple rule:

  • If you record often and care about quality, pick OBS Studio.
  • If you mainly explain things to people, pick Loom.
  • If you are on Windows and love efficient workflows, pick ShareX.
  • If you want a beginner-friendly training recorder, pick ScreenPal.
  • If you only need occasional app capture on Windows, use Xbox Game Bar.

Do not overthink it. Most people get stuck because they choose based on features they will never use. Choose based on what you record every week.

Final Verdict

The best free screen recording tool in 2026 is still OBS Studio if you want the most capability without paying. It has a learning curve, but nothing else on the free side matches its flexibility. For pure convenience, Loom is the better choice. It wins when speed matters more than settings.

If you are deciding between the two, here is the practical answer: OBS is for making content. Loom is for sending communication. Once you know which job you need done, the choice gets easy.

FAQ

What is the best free screen recorder with no watermark?

OBS Studio and ShareX are the strongest free options with no watermark for most users. Xbox Game Bar also avoids watermark issues for basic Windows recording.

Which free screen recorder is best for beginners?

Loom is the easiest for most beginners because the setup is simple and sharing is built in. ScreenPal is also friendly if you want tutorial-style recording.

Can I record gameplay for free?

Yes. OBS Studio is the top free option for gameplay recording, especially if you want higher quality and more control. Xbox Game Bar is fine for simpler Windows gaming captures.

Is Loom free forever?

Loom has a free plan, but the details can change over time. Before relying on it for a full workflow, check the current limits for video length, storage, and sharing.
best-free-screen-recording-tools.ja:
無料の画面録画ツールはたくさんありますが、実際に使ってみると向き不向きがかなりはっきり出ます。チュートリアル作成には強いのにゲーム録画は苦手なものもあれば、共有は一瞬でできるのに細かい設定がほとんど触れないものもあります。2026年時点で定番の無料ツールを実際の用途ベースで比較しました。

結論を先に言うと、総合力で一番強いのはOBS Studioです。チームへの短い説明動画ならLoomが最速です。Windowsでスクリーンショットや録画をまとめて効率化したいならShareXがかなり強いです。何を録るかで選ぶのが正解です。

比較表

ツール向いている用途対応環境無料版の制限使いやすさ
OBS Studio配信、講座、ゲーム録画Windows、Mac、Linux録画時間制限なし中
Loom簡単な説明、チーム共有Web、Windows、Mac、iOS、Android動画時間や保存数に制限ありとても簡単
ShareXWindows作業効率化、注釈付き共有Windows録画時間制限なし中
ScreenPalやさしい操作の教材動画Web、Windows、Mac、Chromebook透かしや機能制限あり簡単
Xbox Game Bar手軽なゲーム録画、アプリ録画WindowsWindows標準搭載とても簡単
Clipchamp録画から簡単編集までWeb、Windows一部出力や素材が制限簡単

今回の比較ポイント

  • 始めやすさ: 初めての人が何分で録画を始められるか
  • 録画の自由度: 全画面、特定ウィンドウ、Webカメラ、マイク、PC音声に対応しているか
  • 画質: 書き出した動画が見やすいか
  • 共有しやすさ: そのまま送れるか、トリミングしやすいか
  • 無料版のストレス: 透かし、時間制限、アカウント必須など

1. OBS Studio、無料で本気ならこれ

OBS Studioは今でも無料の画面録画ツールの本命です。画面、ウィンドウ、Webカメラ、マイク、ブラウザ表示などを重ねて録画でき、チュートリアルも配信もゲーム録画も1本でこなせます。

弱点は最初のハードルです。シーン、ソース、音声ミキサーなど用語が多く、初見では少し重く感じます。ただ、一度設定が決まると毎回同じ品質で録れるので、作業はかなり安定します。

メリット

  • 透かしなし、録画時間制限なし
  • 音声と映像の品質が高い
  • 複数素材を重ねた本格的な構成ができる
  • 配信と録画の両方に強い
  • Windows、Mac、Linux対応

デメリット

  • 初心者には少し難しい
  • 設定項目が多い
  • クラウド共有は標準で弱い
  • 編集機能はほぼ別ソフト前提

向いている人: YouTube投稿、講座作成、ゲーム録画などを継続的にやる人。

2. Loom、すぐ撮ってすぐ送るなら最速

Loomの強みは制作機能ではなく、説明の速さです。画面とカメラを選んで録画し、終わったらそのままリンク共有。これが本当に速いです。社内説明、クライアント報告、バグ報告にはかなり相性がいいです。

メリット

  • 録画から共有までが最速クラス
  • 初心者でも迷いにくい
  • カメラ付き説明動画が作りやすい
  • チーム共有に向いている

デメリット

  • 無料版は動画時間や保存数に制限があることが多い
  • 細かい画質設定は弱い
  • ゲーム録画には向かない
  • アカウント前提の運用になる

向いている人: チーム連携、営業、サポート、リモートワーク中心の人。

3. ShareX、Windowsの効率化ツールとして強い

ShareXは録画専用ソフトというより、キャプチャ全般の万能ツールです。スクリーンショット、GIF、短い画面録画、注釈、アップロード自動化までまとめてこなせます。Windowsで作業速度を上げたい人にはかなり便利です。

メリット

  • 無料で機能が非常に多い
  • スクリーンショット注釈が強い
  • 自動化設定が豊富
  • 開発や検証作業と相性がいい

デメリット

  • Windows専用
  • 少し技術寄りの画面
  • 動画編集は弱い
  • 最適化まで少し設定が必要

向いている人: 開発者、QA、サポート担当、Windowsヘビーユーザー。

4. ScreenPal、教材動画をやさしく作りたい人向け

ScreenPalはOBSほど重くなく、Loomより教材向け機能がそろっています。授業動画、社内マニュアル、操作説明を作る用途では扱いやすいです。

メリット

  • 初心者でも理解しやすい
  • 教育、説明動画との相性がいい
  • 基本的な編集やナレーション機能がある
  • 複数環境で使いやすい

デメリット

  • 無料版の制限はやや気になる
  • 透かしやブランド表示が入る場合がある
  • OBSほど自由度はない
  • 軽快さはツールによって上がる

向いている人: 教師、社内研修担当、小規模チーム。

5. Xbox Game Bar、Windows標準で一番手軽

Windowsなら追加インストールなしで使えるのがXbox Game Barです。ショートカットですぐ起動でき、アプリやゲームの録画をサッと始められます。

メリット

  • Windowsに最初から入っている
  • 起動が速い
  • 簡単な録画には十分
  • 余計な学習コストが少ない

デメリット

  • 本格的なチュートリアル制作には弱い
  • 機能はかなり限定的
  • 複数ソースを重ねる用途には向かない
  • Windowsのみ

向いている人: たまに録るだけのWindowsユーザー。

6. Clipchamp、録画と軽い編集を1か所で

Clipchampは録画後の簡単なカット編集まで同じ画面で進めたい人に向いています。短い解説動画やSNS向け動画ならこの手軽さは大きいです。

メリット

  • 録画と編集をまとめてできる
  • 画面が見やすい
  • 短尺動画に向いている
  • 初心者でも進めやすい

デメリット

  • 一部機能や素材は有料寄り
  • OBSほど高機能ではない
  • PC性能が弱いとWeb版が重いことがある

向いている人: 初心者、マーケ担当、ひとり運営の発信者。

用途別おすすめ

用途おすすめ理由
本格チュートリアルOBS Studio画質と構成の自由度が高い
チーム共有Loom共有までが圧倒的に速い
ゲーム録画OBS Studio安定して高品質
Windows効率化ShareX録画とスクショを一体化できる
教育動画ScreenPal説明動画向けで使いやすい
インストールなしで録るXbox Game Bar標準搭載ですぐ使える

選び方の結論

  • 画質重視で長く使うなら OBS Studio
  • 説明動画をすぐ送りたいなら Loom
  • Windowsで効率化したいなら ShareX
  • やさしく始めたいなら ScreenPal
  • たまに録るだけなら Xbox Game Bar

機能表だけで選ぶと失敗しやすいです。毎週どんな動画を録るのかで決めるとハズしません。

最終判断

2026年の無料画面録画ツールで一番おすすめしやすいのは、やはりOBS Studioです。覚えることは少しありますが、無料でここまでできるツールは他にありません。共有スピードを最優先するならLoomの方が合っています。

FAQ

透かしなしの無料画面録画ツールはありますか？

あります。OBS Studio、ShareX、Xbox Game Barは透かしなしで使いやすい代表格です。

初心者に一番おすすめなのはどれですか？

一番わかりやすいのはLoomです。録って送る流れがとても簡単です。

無料でゲーム録画もできますか？

できます。品質と安定性まで考えるならOBS Studioが一番強いです。

Loomの無料版だけで足りますか？

短い説明動画中心なら足りることが多いです。ただし保存数や時間制限は事前に確認した方が安全です。
best-free-screen-recording-tools.de:
Kostenlose Bildschirmrekorder gibt es viele. Das Problem ist nicht die Auswahl, sondern die falsche Auswahl. Manche Tools sind stark für Tutorials, aber schwach bei Spielen. Andere sind perfekt für kurze Erklärvideos, stoßen aber bei längeren Aufnahmen schnell an Grenzen. Wir haben die bekanntesten kostenlosen Screen-Recorder 2026 getestet und nach echten Einsatzzwecken bewertet.

Das Ergebnis ist klar: OBS Studio bleibt die beste kostenlose Lösung für alle, die Qualität und Kontrolle wollen. Loom ist die schnellste Option für kurze Demos und Team-Kommunikation. ShareX ist für Windows-Nutzer mit produktivem Workflow besonders stark. Welches Tool richtig ist, hängt davon ab, was Sie jede Woche aufnehmen.

Schnellvergleich

ToolAm besten fürPlattformKostenlose LimitsBedienung
OBS StudioTutorials, Streaming, GamingWindows, Mac, LinuxKein ZeitlimitMittel
LoomSchnelle Erklärvideos, Team-UpdatesWeb, Windows, Mac, iOS, AndroidBegrenzte Videolänge oder BibliothekSehr einfach
ShareXWindows-Workflows, annotierte AufnahmenWindowsKein ZeitlimitMittel
ScreenPalEinfache SchulungsvideosWeb, Windows, Mac, ChromebookWasserzeichen und FunktionslimitsEinfach
Xbox Game BarSpiele und App-AufnahmenWindowsIn Windows integriertSehr einfach
ClipchampAufnehmen und direkt schneidenWeb, WindowsExport- und Premium-LimitsEinfach

So haben wir getestet

  • Startgeschwindigkeit: Wie schnell kann ein Anfänger die erste Aufnahme machen?
  • Flexibilität: Bildschirm, Fenster, Webcam, Mikrofon und Systemaudio
  • Qualität: Wie sauber wirkt das exportierte Video?
  • Teilen: Wie einfach ist Zuschneiden, Export oder Link-Freigabe?
  • Reibung im Gratisplan: Wasserzeichen, Zeitlimits, Kontopflicht, gesperrte Funktionen

1. OBS Studio, die beste Gratislösung für ernsthafte Arbeit

OBS Studio ist weiterhin der leistungsstärkste kostenlose Bildschirmrekorder. Sie können Displays, einzelne Fenster, Browserquellen, Kamera und Mikrofon kombinieren und daraus saubere Aufnahmen oder Streams bauen. Für Kurse, YouTube, Demos und Gameplay ist OBS nach wie vor die Referenz.

Vorteile

  • Kein Wasserzeichen und kein Zeitlimit
  • Sehr gute Video- und Audioqualität
  • Flexible Szenen und Overlays
  • Für Streaming und lokale Aufnahmen geeignet
  • Windows, Mac und Linux werden unterstützt

Nachteile

  • Steilere Lernkurve
  • Für Einsteiger anfangs unübersichtlich
  • Kein integrierter Cloud-Share-Workflow
  • Videoschnitt nur sehr begrenzt vorhanden

Für wen geeignet: Content Creator, Trainer, Streamer und alle, die regelmäßig aufnehmen.

2. Loom, am besten für schnelle Kommunikation

Loom ist nicht für aufwendige Produktion gebaut, sondern für Geschwindigkeit. Aufnahme starten, etwas erklären, Link verschicken, fertig. Genau deshalb ist es für Remote-Teams, Kunden-Updates und kurze Produktdemos so beliebt.

Vorteile

  • Extrem schneller Aufnahme-und-Teilen-Workflow
  • Sehr leicht zu bedienen
  • Webcam und Mikrofon sind schnell eingerichtet
  • Ideal für Zusammenarbeit im Team

Nachteile

  • Gratisplan hat je nach Angebot Grenzen bei Länge oder Speicher
  • Weniger Kontrolle als OBS
  • Für Gaming kaum geeignet
  • Account-basierter Workflow

Für wen geeignet: Teams, Freelancer, Support, Vertrieb und alle, die oft etwas kurz erklären müssen.

3. ShareX, die beste Wahl für produktive Windows-Nutzer

ShareX ist mehr als ein Recorder. Screenshots, GIFs, kurze Videos, Upload-Automation und Annotationen laufen in einem Werkzeug zusammen. Gerade für Windows-Nutzer, die effizient arbeiten wollen, ist das stark.

Vorteile

  • Kostenlos, Open Source und sehr leistungsfähig
  • Starke Screenshot- und Annotationsfunktionen
  • Viele Automatisierungen möglich
  • Gut für technische Workflows

Nachteile

  • Nur für Windows
  • Teilweise technisch wirkende Oberfläche
  • Videoschnitt kaum vorhanden
  • Erst nach Konfiguration richtig stark

Für wen geeignet: Entwickler, QA-Teams, Support und Power-User unter Windows.

4. ScreenPal, gut für einfache Lern- und Schulungsvideos

ScreenPal ist ein freundlicher Mittelweg. Einfacher als OBS, aber stärker auf Lehr- und Anleitungsvideos ausgelegt als ganz einfache Recorder. Für Schulen, interne Dokumentation oder Einsteiger-Tutorials ist das praktisch.

Vorteile

  • Einsteigerfreundlich
  • Geeignet für Schulungen und Erklärvideos
  • Grundlegende Bearbeitung und Sprachaufnahme enthalten
  • Auf mehreren Geräten nutzbar

Nachteile

  • Gratislimits sind spürbar
  • Je nach Workflow Wasserzeichen oder Branding
  • Weniger flexibel als OBS
  • Wirkt teils träger als leichtere Tools

Für wen geeignet: Lehrer, Trainer und kleine Teams mit einfachen Schulungsaufnahmen.

5. Xbox Game Bar, die schnellste eingebaute Windows-Lösung

Wer unter Windows einfach nur schnell eine App oder ein Spiel aufnehmen will, bekommt mit der Xbox Game Bar die direkteste Lösung. Kein Download, keine Registrierung, keine komplizierte Einrichtung.

Vorteile

  • Bereits in Windows enthalten
  • Sehr schnell startklar
  • Für einfache Aufnahmen ausreichend
  • Kein zusätzlicher Installationsaufwand

Nachteile

  • Für professionelle Tutorials zu eingeschränkt
  • Wenig Funktionen
  • Nicht für komplexe Szenen gedacht
  • Nur Windows

Für wen geeignet: Alle, die gelegentlich und ohne Extra-Software aufnehmen wollen.

6. Clipchamp, gut wenn Aufnahme und Schnitt zusammengehören

Clipchamp ist sinnvoll, wenn Sie nicht nur aufnehmen, sondern direkt im gleichen Tool kürzen und bearbeiten möchten. Für kurze Social-Videos und einfache Erklärclips ist das angenehm.

Vorteile

  • Aufnahme und Schnitt in einem Tool
  • Saubere und verständliche Oberfläche
  • Gut für kurze Erklärvideos
  • Einsteigerfreundlich

Nachteile

  • Einige Funktionen und Assets sind kostenpflichtig
  • Nicht so flexibel wie OBS
  • Web-Workflow kann auf schwächeren Geräten langsamer sein

Für wen geeignet: Anfänger, Marketer und Solo-Creator mit einfachen Bearbeitungsanforderungen.

Beste Wahl je nach Einsatz

EinsatzBeste WahlWarum
TutorialsOBS StudioBeste Qualität und Szenenkontrolle
Team-UpdatesLoomSchnellstes Teilen
GamingOBS StudioStabile Aufnahme in hoher Qualität
